mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods yield negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend levels signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive GDP surprises produce negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and stable consumer price inflation creates an optimal discount rate environment that sustains positive equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL spread fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 confirm robust labor demand that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in >60% of months where payrolls exceed the 200k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects strong household purchasing power that directly supports corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases.
